mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and raise equity discount rates, directly compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains above 4.5% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital expenditure expansion, generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive shocks to retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during an easing cycle reflects accommodative financing enabling factory expansion, which translates to immediate positive market momentum over one week.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production changes during easing periods yield negative 5-day returns in > 60% of observations, or regime-specific alpha vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend confirms macroeconomic expansion, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and yielding positive forward equity returns over three months.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or 60-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with quarterly GDP revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPI growth above 3% forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity multiples and causing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPI exceeds the 3% threshold, or the signal fails to predict returns when inflation is supply-driven rather than demand-driven.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment creates a tight labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a late-cycle expansion signal deteriorating consumer confidence and reduced construction activity, triggering immediate negative market returns over one week.
failure_condition: Negative housing starts during late-cycle phases fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or the mechanism breaks down when housing declines are driven by seasonal adjustments rather than fundamentals.
